mnemonics: CAR: Copy, Assign, Reference. Assignment copies the state, but references just point.
keyPoints: Object assignment uses the assignment operator (=) to copy the state from one object to another
The compiler provides a default assignment operator that performs member-wise copying
For classes with dynamically allocated memory, you must define your own copy assignment operator to prevent shallow copies and memory leaks
commonMistakes: Using assignment (=) when initialization (constructor) is intended, especially with references
Forgetting to handle self-assignment (obj = obj) in custom assignment operators
Not implementing the Rule of Three: if you need a custom destructor, copy constructor, or copy assignment operator, you likely need all three
